assertions.expectation: Explains the difference between Rapid and Stable channels (Stable gets versions later, more validated)
assertions.reason: Response clearly explains that Stable versions arrive 8-12 weeks later after validation in Regular channel, while Rapid gets versions first

assertions.expectation: Notes that channel changes don't trigger an immediate upgrade or downgrade
assertions.reason: Response explicitly states 'Change release channel (no immediate version change)' in the migration command section

assertions.expectation: Warns that if 1.30 isn't yet available on Stable, the cluster may not receive patches until Stable catches up
assertions.reason: Response identifies this as a 'Critical decision point' and warns that if 1.30.x isn't available in Stable, they need to either wait or downgrade

assertions.expectation: Provides the gcloud command to change the release channel
assertions.reason: Response provides the exact command: 'gcloud container clusters update CLUSTER_NAME --zone ZONE --release-channel stable'

assertions.expectation: Recommends checking version availability on Stable before switching (gcloud container get-server-config)
assertions.reason: Response includes specific commands to check version availability in Stable channel using gcloud container get-server-config

assertions.expectation: Discusses the impact on auto-upgrade timing going forward
assertions.reason: Response explains that auto-upgrades will happen within maintenance windows but following Stable's schedule, and discusses new upgrade timeline expectations

assertions.expectation: Mentions that the Extended channel is an option if they need even slower cadence (with cost implications)
assertions.reason: Response mentions 'Extended support (24 months) available on Stable if needed for compliance' but doesn't discuss Extended channel as an alternative release channel option

assertions.expectation: Advises coordinating the change during a maintenance window
assertions.reason: Response recommends executing during scheduled maintenance window and provides specific gcloud command with maintenance window parameters
